session_artifacts.cycle_brief_md:
# Config Tuning Session â€” cycle_2026-04

## Scope

Two of the six D5 configs retuned this session:

- `signal_thresholds` (v3)
- `margin_config` (v2)

The remaining four (`scoring_thresholds`, `momentum_thresholds`,
`window_config`, `premium_config`) were reviewed and left unchanged.

## signal_thresholds v3

Raised the Strong-signal floor from **7% â†’ 8% net margin**.

**Rationale:** Q1 data showed the Strong tier clustering near the
floor. Tightening the cutoff keeps the label meaningful rather than
label-creep toward averages.

| Tier | Old | **New** |
|---|---|---|
| Strong | â‰Ą 7% | **â‰Ą 8%** |
| Normal | â‰Ą 5% | 5% (unchanged) |
| Weak | â‰¤ 3% | 3% (unchanged) |

## margin_config v2

Lowered the **Grailzee-only** deal-kill floor from **2% â†’ 1.5% net**.
eBay and Chrono24 unchanged.

**Rationale:** Grailzee dropped platform fees in April. A 2% margin
there is now structurally cleaner than a 2% margin on eBay was last
quarter. We want to capture the marginal flips without loosening
the standard elsewhere.

| Platform | Old floor | **New floor** |
|---|---|---|
| Grailzee | 2.0% | **1.5%** |
| eBay | 2.5% | 2.5% |
| Chrono24 | 3.0% | 3.0% |

## Follow-ups

- Revisit in 30 days to confirm the Grailzee floor drop is pulling
  in clean deals and not just noisy ones.
- `momentum_thresholds` is next candidate for review; not enough Q1
  signal yet to justify a change.
